ideas.headline: Strong agentic loop and a permanent civil-service buyer (ΓΓΚ), but the marquee Redbox precedent collapsed (i.AI sunset, Dec 2025) and the studioFit penalty for the most politically-proximate ministry is real.
blockers: Headline precedent (UK Redbox) was sunset by i.AI in December 2025; DBT migrated to 'Assist' as a one-off and Cabinet Office transitioned to a gemini-based enterprise option. The 'we are the Greek Redbox' pitch no longer lands — re-frame on Assist/Gemini-Enterprise/Assistant IA generation precedents.
ΓΓΚ is a permanent role but warm intro through T-Life's network is not currently in hand; one or two retired ΓΓΚ briefing officers must be retained as paid advisors before any approach.

PM-office-proximate work means relationship is partially exposed to the political weather even when contracting with the apolitical Secretariat — a single 'AI writes the PM's briefings' headline can torch the channel.
Founder bench: ex-ΓΓΚ briefing officer recruitable but small; talent search needs to start now.
ideas.next30days: Map and approach two retired ΓΓΚ briefing officers as paid founding advisors; commission a sanitised side-by-side demo (mock bilateral brief, hand-written vs. agent-drafted) using public-domain inputs only, to be shown to the first advisor as the credentialing artefact.

ideas.headline: Best Επικρατείας bet — Council Secretariat seat is structurally apolitical, the EU AI Act high-risk deadline slipped to Dec 2027 buying design runway, and Estonia's e-Cabinet + 2026 Bürokratt agentic layer gives a fresh sovereign precedent we can cite.
blockers: Classified-handling carve-out (subset of Υπουργικό Συμβούλιο papers) forces on-prem or sovereign-cloud — pushes the build cost above the €30k direct-award threshold immediately, so the procurement story must rely on the innovation-pilot path or an RRF e-government envelope from day one.
Founder mix is three-headed (ex-ΓΓΚ Council Secretariat officer + applied-NLP engineer + classified-IT partner) — three-person assembly is fragile and slower than the two-person archetype.

Redbox+Minute as a single marquee precedent is degraded (Minute persists at DBT-as-Assist, Redbox sunset). Need to lead with Estonia e-Cabinet + Bürokratt 2026 and France's Assistant IA pilot instead.
Pilot budget (€180-350k) is above απευθείας ανάθεση (€30k services); needs an innovation-pilot framing or RRF envelope to procure cleanly.
ideas.next30days: Secure one retired ΓΓΚ Council Secretariat officer as paid founding advisor; draft a one-pager mapping the Greek Υπουργικό Συμβούλιο procedural code onto the Estonia e-Cabinet + Bürokratt agentic layer as the canonical precedent (replacing the Redbox+Minute citation in the original memo); identify two classified-IT-handling partners (likely existing Greek SI or sovereign-cloud vendor) and run one

ideas.headline: Highest-moat angle in the portfolio and the apolitical Bureau seat survives government changes, but the founder constraint (ex-ΣτΕ rapporteur or ex-Bureau νομοτέχνης) is the binding bottleneck — without that bench, this idea cannot ship.
blockers: Senior public-law jurist bench in Athens is one of the smallest founder pools in Greek govtech — without a named ex-Bureau νομοτέχνης or ex-ΣτΕ πάρεδρος on the cap table, the pitch is dead on arrival. Talent search is the gating activity.

EU AI Act high-risk classification arguably applies to legislative-drafting tools; the May 7 2026 Omnibus deal defers Annex III enforcement to Dec 2027, which buys runway, but pilots after that date carry conformity-assessment overhead from day one — design must bake this in.

Precedent set is thinner than originally implied: France Albert's France Services rollout was not generalised; Albert API persists and 'Assistant IA' is a 10k-agent pilot through June 2026, but neither is a legislative-drafting deployment. UK GDS 'Lex' remains exploratory. The 'this is being done elsewhere already' frame collapses on close inspection — re-frame as genuine first-mover instead.
Pilot budget (€150-300k) exceeds απευθείας ανάθεση, requires innovation-pilot sole-source justification on Greek public-law specificity — defensible but not automatic.
ideas.next30days: Run a structured founder search: list every retired or near-retirement Bureau νομοτέχνης and ex-ΣτΕ πάρεδρος of the last 10 years; secure paid feasibility study (<€10k) with one candidate to validate corpus access (Bureau drafting manual, αιτιολογική-έκθεση archive) and produce a single annotated demo on a real recent νομοσχέδιο.

ideas.headline: Hostile pass confirms the original park hardens to fail: the artefact is a structurally leak-prone catalogue of broken government promises, and the buyer (Minister-of-State office) is the politically most-volatile seat in the ministry — studioFit collapses on the same logic as the statement-consistency monitor.
blockers: Champion is the Secretary General of Coordination at the PM-office — political appointee, replaced at every government change. lockInRisk drops accordingly; this is not a permanent-civil-service seat like ΓΓΚ proper.

The artefact's value IS the politically explosive output: a ranked list of which Secretaries-General are missing their commitments. A single screenshot leak burns the vendor across every other Επικρατείας idea — same structural class of risk as the statement-consistency monitor.
'Positive framing' (praise/push tool) does not survive contact with the actual artefact; first internal demo will show ministers their own slippage, and the leak countdown starts immediately.
Pilot procurement (€120-250k, on-prem) is above απευθείας ανάθεση and requires sovereign-cloud carve-out, but the deeper issue is the buyer's political risk appetite — not procurement.

StudioFit penalty: T-Life Capital's portfolio diversification depends on not having a single politically-exposed product torch the relationship for ΓΓΚ Council-prep and Bureau-copilot. This idea concentrates that risk.
ideas.next30days: Do not pursue as a studio venture. Revisit only if Greece sets up a permanent civil-service Delivery Unit (analogous to the 2025 UK 10DU institutionalisation under the Chief Secretary to the PM) with a named non-political career-civil-service lead — that would change the buyer seat and the lockInRisk profile. Until then, leak-risk dominates.

ideas.headline: Original kill confirmed on hostile pass. Included in the top-5 cut per instruction; studioFit scored at floor because the artefact is structurally a leak-bomb whose single-screenshot exposure incinerates every other Επικρατείας channel the studio is building.
blockers: The product's value IS the explosive artefact — a ranked dashboard of inter-cabinet contradictions. No design fix removes this; obfuscation makes the tool useless to the buyer.

International precedent is the weakest in the portfolio: UK No.10 'grid' is a manual coordination function with no automation precedent; Israel PMO tooling is undisclosed. Nothing to cite as a fresh deployment in 2025-2026.
Buyer is the Minister-of-State political appointee — replaced every government change, so any pilot's institutional memory dies with the government. lockInRisk is at floor.

Single screenshot leak from this dashboard hands the opposition a constitutional-crisis press cycle and torches T-Life's relationship across every other Επικρατείας venture in flight (Council-prep, Bureau-copilot, dossier agent). StudioFit at floor — this is anti-portfolio.
Founder pitch implodes on first principles: no senior public-administration professional in Athens will let this carry their name.
ideas.next30days: Do not pursue. No revisit path — the kill is structural, not solvable by re-scoping. If a similar workflow ever ships in Greek government, it will be built in-house by the PMO comms team without external vendor exposure.
